 {"id":"wa-5mpu","title":"Design: schema→docs/types strategy (direction, versioning, languages)","description":"# Task: Decide schema-driven generation strategy

## Goal
Choose the practical strategy for schema-driven docs and typed clients.

## Requirements
- Decide generation direction:
  - Rust structs → JSON Schema (preferred for single source of truth)
  - OR JSON Schema → types (and keep schemas authored by hand)
- Decide client target(s):
  - TypeScript (common for tooling)
  - Python (common for scripts)
  - Rust client crate (for in-repo safety)
- Decide versioning policy:
  - how schema versions map to wa versions
  - how breaking changes are detected

## Acceptance Criteria
- Strategy is chosen and consistent with repo goals (robust, low tech debt).
","notes":"Decision documented in PLAN.md section 21.5; ready to close once parent unblocked.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T20:48:47.647300556Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.245213-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-07T00:19:50.945306944Z"}

 {"id":"wa-5oq5","title":"Environment detection: detect WezTerm, agents, config state for contextual exercises","description":"# Environment detection for tutorial

## Purpose
Detect the user's environment to provide contextual exercises and skip irrelevant steps. A user without WezTerm running shouldn't do "start watching" exercises.

## Background
Different users have different setups:
- WezTerm running vs not
- Agents present vs empty panes
- wa configured vs fresh install
- Existing DB vs first time

The tutorial should adapt to each situation.

## Implementation Details

### Environment Checks
```rust
pub struct TutorialEnvironment {
    wezterm_running: bool,
    wezterm_version: Option<String>,
    pane_count: usize,
    agent_panes: Vec<AgentInfo>, // Panes with detected agents
    wa_configured: bool,         // wa.toml exists
    db_has_data: bool,           // Segments/events exist
    shell_integration: bool,     // OSC 133 markers detected
}

impl TutorialEnvironment {
    pub async fn detect() -> Self { ... }
    
    pub fn can_run_exercise(&self, exercise: &Exercise) -> CanRun {
        match exercise.requirements {
            Requires::WeztermRunning if !self.wezterm_running => 
                CanRun::No("Start WezTerm first"),
            Requires::AgentPresent if self.agent_panes.is_empty() => 
                CanRun::Simulation("No agents detected, using simulation"),
            _ => CanRun::Yes,
        }
    }
}
```

### Exercise Requirements
Each exercise declares its requirements:
```rust
pub struct Exercise {
    id: ExerciseId,
    title: String,
    requirements: Vec<Requirement>,
    can_simulate: bool,  // True if exercise works in sandbox mode
}
```

### Adaptive Flow
1. User starts track
2. Detect environment
3. For each exercise:
   - Check requirements
   - If met: run exercise
   - If not met but can_simulate: run in sandbox
   - If not met and can't simulate: skip with explanation

## Testing
- Unit tests for each detection check
- Mock tests for different environment states
- Integration tests with real WezTerm (optional, CI skip)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] TutorialEnvironment struct with all detection fields
- [ ] detect() function checks all conditions
- [ ] can_run_exercise() returns appropriate guidance
- [ ] Clear user messaging for skipped exercises
- [ ] Tests for all detection paths","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:45:59.975507421Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.203549-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-07T07:22:49.569386524Z"}
-{"id":"wa-5rj6","title":"LRU cache — bounded O(1) cache with arena-based doubly-linked list","description":"Generic LRU cache using HashMap + arena-indexed doubly-linked list. All operations O(1). No unsafe code (arena indices instead of pointers). Features: get/put/peek/remove, capacity management, eviction callback, stats (hits/misses/evictions). Supports the existing ad-hoc LRU in ingest.rs and general caching needs for pane snapshots, search results, session data.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-12T03:09:27.95307-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T03:09:31.958974-05:00"}

No unsafe code (arena indices instead of pointers). Features: get/put/peek/remove, capacity management, eviction callback, stats (hits/misses/evictions). Supports the existing ad-hoc LRU in ingest.rs and general caching needs for pane snapshots, search results, session data.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-12T03:09:27.95307-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T03:15:22.739977-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T03:15:22.739977-05:00","close_reason":"LRU cache module: arena-based O(1) operations, 40 tests passing. Committed as 8529ab3d."}
 {"id":"wa-5wge","title":"Secret scan engine (reuse redactor patterns)","description":"## What
Implement a scanner that reuses redaction patterns to detect secrets in stored segments.

## Why
We need a deterministic, safe way to assess exposure without printing secrets.

## How
- Stream segments from storage, run redaction patterns
- Count matches and record redacted snippets (hashes only)

## Success Criteria
- Scanner never emits raw secret values
- Performance is acceptable on large DBs","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:16:20.128394062Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T01:34:50.355736-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-11T01:34:50.355744-05:00"}
 {"id":"wa-5y7","title":"Unit tests for ingest pipeline","description":"## Tests Required
1. Delta extraction algorithm
2. Overlap matching edge cases
3. Sequence number assignment
4. Gap detection scenarios
5. Backpressure behavior
6. Adaptive polling timing

## Property-Based Tests
- Segments never skip sequence numbers (unless gap recorded)
- Full content is captured when no gaps

## Acceptance
- `cargo test ingest` passes
- All edge cases covered","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T08:56:21.076645438Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T08:57:09.509134347Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T08:57:09.509134347Z","close_reason":"Redundant - testing already covered by wa-4vx.10 and component test tasks","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-5y7","depends_on_id":"wa-0je","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T04:09:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
